status: ACTIVE PLAN - supersedes the To-Be rebuild as the current path. The To-Be spec and Design Review remain the long-term north star.
goals: Attribution correct: first-touch preserved, click + intake SRA + consult SRA frozen per deal, Meta/Google conversion sends keyed to the deal
Opportunity disposition correct: every sale/renewal is its own opportunity carrying its own outcome, PCC, provider, products, amounts, and dates - renewals stop overwriting history
non_goals: No workflow rebuild, no adoption of the 63 vendor drafts, no To-Be cutover
No changes to live nurtures, reminders, routing, or messaging
No contact-field retirement - every live workflow keeps reading the exact fields and values it reads today
No 11,000-contact migration (renewal_date backfill for ACTIVE members only, Phase 6)
core_mechanism: DUAL-WRITE. The live workflows all read and write contact fields - they are untouched and keep firing on the exact same fields and string values as today. The layer we already control (PCC disposition form app + intake webhook + booking/reconciler) additionally writes the permanent per-deal record onto the opportunity in the same API call. Contact = latest-values scratchpad for the live automations. Opportunity = immutable per-deal truth for reporting, attribution, and conversions. The live build never notices; the reporting layer stops lying.

phases.title: Phase 0 - Prerequisites and settings
phases.objective: API access, safety snapshot, and the duplicates toggle - in the right order.

steps.title: Private Integration Token
steps.detail: Sub-account Settings > Private Integrations > create token with scopes: contacts.readonly, contacts.write, opportunities.readonly, opportunities.write, forms.readonly, locations/customFields.readonly + write. Store as GHL_PIT in the admin app environment (Vercel env var, never in code). This is the writer identity for every API call below.

steps.title: Safety snapshot
steps.detail: Run the extraction harness against the live sub-account (workflows, custom fields, custom values, pipelines, forms) and commit the JSON to the mwc-workflow-mapper repo. This is the rollback reference and the before-picture for the conformance diff.

steps.title: Ship the create-once guard FIRST
steps.detail: Deploy the app-side guard (Phase 4.1) before flipping any toggle: with Allow Duplicate Opportunities ON, GHL stops collapsing repeat creates into updates, so the app guard becomes the only dedupe. Guard first, toggle second, same day.

steps.title: Enable Allow Duplicate Opportunities
steps.detail: Sub-account Settings > Pipelines (Opportunities settings) > toggle 'Allow Duplicate Opportunities' ON. The setting has moved between GHL releases - if not under Pipelines, check the Opportunities board settings gear. Effect: one contact can hold multiple deals, which is the entire point. Never toggle this back OFF once multiple deals exist per contact (upsert semantics change underneath the live build).

phases.title: Phase 1 - Opportunity custom fields
phases.objective: Create the per-deal field set. Settings > Custom Fields > select object Opportunity > add each field, or POST via the custom-fields API with model=opportunity. Keys must match exactly (lowercase snake_case).

rows: sold | nosale | mut | mar - lowercase codes, exact
text, not dropdown - staff lists drift; the app validates against its own roster
TRT | HRT | GLP1 | Combo | ICP | ED | B Complex
INTEGER months: 1,3,6,12,24,30,36,42 - prose stays in form labels
MONETARY fields are writable via API (not via workflow actions - known platform limit, irrelevant here since the app writes)
computed by the app: sold date + term_1_months
frozen at create; keys to conversion uploads (Phase 5)
channel code at create: paid_search | paid_social | organic | gbp | referral | phone | chat | walkin
ISO timestamp idempotency stamp - written by the app after a successful disposition commit

steps.title: Contact fields - add only three
steps.detail: contact.sra_intake_source (single select, 13 codes), contact.sra_intake_source_detail (text), contact.sra_intake_at (datetime). These are NEW keys no live workflow reads, so they are safe. Every other contact field already exists and keeps its exact current key, type, and option strings - do not touch them.

phases.title: Phase 2 - PCC disposition form changes
phases.objective: The form app becomes the single writer of the per-deal record while keeping its legacy contact writes byte-identical.

steps.title: Add the consult SRA question
steps.detail: New required question on the showed path, positioned after Patient Type: 'How did you hear about us?' - single select of the 13 labels (Phase 3 table), plus a free-text detail input that appears when the answer is referral or other. PCC asks verbally and picks the closest. Writes opportunity.sra_consult_source + _detail. If the live intake already captured an answer, prefill the selector with it and let the PCC confirm or change - the two answers are SUPPOSED to disagree sometimes; that disagreement is the triangulation signal.

steps.title: Write-order contract (exact sequence per submit)
steps.detail: 1) Resolve the deal: search open opportunities in the Sales pipeline for the contact (GET /opportunities/search?location_id=...&contact_id=...&status=open). Exactly one open deal expected; zero -> create one now with the Phase 4 attribution stamp (walk-in/phone edge); two+ -> flag to the reconciler, use newest. 2) PUT the opportunity: ALL custom fields from the table, monetaryValue = NET collected (after discounts), and status/stage (won on sold, lost on nosale/mut, open on mar) in ONE API call - stage moves are API-side only, never workflow-side. 3) POST the contact update with the EXACT legacy payload the form sends today - same keys, same casing, same prose values (if the live build stores 'SOLD' or '3 mo' on the contact, keep sending 'SOLD' and '3 mo'; the lowercase codes live on the opportunity only). This is what keeps every live workflow firing unchanged. 4) Compute renewal_date = sold date + term_1_months (app-side date math) and write it to BOTH contact.renewal_date (legacy readers) and opportunity.renewal_date. 5) Stamp opportunity.outcome_processed_at = now ISO on success.

steps.title: Idempotency and edits
steps.detail: Before writing, read opportunity.outcome_processed_at. Empty -> full commit including conversion signals (Phase 5). Set -> this is an EDIT: update fields, refresh contact copies, but suppress every conversion re-fire and stage re-move. A resubmitted form must never double-count a sale or re-send a Purchase event.

steps.title: Dual-write map (contact legacy key -> opportunity key)
steps.detail: sale outcome -> sale_outcome (code-mapped) | sale type / new-or-renewal -> sale_type | pay type -> pay_type (code-mapped) | PCC -> patient_care_consultant | total program amount -> total_program_amount | treatment_cost (legacy Price 1) -> price_1 | price__item_2 -> price_2 | term_length__item_1/2 (prose) -> term_1_months/term_2_months (parse '3 mo' -> 3) | treatment_type (legacy Product 1) -> product_1 | product_sold__item_2 -> product_2 | consultation_fee -> consultation_fee | provider_making_recommendation -> provider | location stays contact-level (deal inherits via pipeline reporting). The value conversion happens in ONE app-side constants table so the mapping has a single home.

phases.title: Phase 3 - Intake survey changes
phases.objective: Port the existing intake attribution question onto the deal without touching what the live build reads.

steps.title: Keep the live question, standardize the code list
steps.detail: The intake survey already asks how they heard about us. Keep the question and its existing answer wording UNCHANGED in the UI (live workflow branches may match those exact strings). Server-side, map each existing answer to the canonical code. If an existing option has no home in the 13 codes, map to other + detail rather than adding codes.

steps.title: Canonical SRA codes (both intake and consult)
steps.detail: google = Google search | maps = Google Maps | facebook = Facebook | instagram = Instagram | youtube = YouTube | tiktok = TikTok | tv = TV / streaming (MNTN) | radio = Radio | billboard = Billboard / sign | podcast = Podcast | referral = Friend / member referral | review = Online review | other = Other. Lowercase codes are stored; labels are display-only.

steps.title: Webhook handler additions
steps.detail: On intake completion the handler (in addition to everything it does today, unchanged): 1) writes contact.sra_intake_source = code, contact.sra_intake_source_detail, contact.sra_intake_at = now ISO; 2) keeps writing whatever legacy contact field the live build stores the answer in, byte-identical; 3) stamps the newest OPEN Sales opportunity with opportunity.sra_intake_source (search open by contact; if none exists yet - intake completed before booking - skip, the Phase 2 disposition write backfills sra_intake_source from the contact at consult time); 4) fires the Meta CompleteRegistration event via the relay with event_id = '{opportunityId}:intake' when a deal exists (Phase 5).

phases.title: Phase 4 - Attribution stamp at opportunity create
phases.objective: Every deal is born carrying its own frozen attribution.

steps.title: Create-once guard
steps.detail: Single app-side function owns ALL opportunity creates (booking flow, disposition edge-case create, reconciler). Rule: if an OPEN Sales-pipeline opportunity exists for the contact -> return it, never create a second. No open deal (brand-new lead, or returning member whose prior deal is won/lost) -> create. A renewal correctly gets a NEW deal because the prior one is closed.

steps.title: Stamp at create
steps.detail: The create call includes customFields: utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, gclid, fbclid (from the contact's current last-touch values at that moment) + lead_source (channel code). This is the freeze: later touches update the contact's last-touch fields but NEVER the deal's copy. A renewal deal created months later gets the attribution of the renewal moment (usually direct/CRM) - correct by design; the original ad's credit lives untouched on deal #1.

steps.title: first_* write-once, enforced app-side
steps.detail: The app never overwrites contact first_utm_*, first_source, first_ad_id: write only if empty. Live workflows that set them keep doing whatever they do today - the app just refuses to be a second writer.

steps.title: Reconciler backfill
steps.detail: The existing 15-minute reconciler adds one check: any open opportunity missing utm/gclid/fbclid/lead_source stamps gets backfilled from the contact's first_* (preferred) or current last-touch, and logs the gap.

phases.title: Phase 5 - Meta and Google conversion sends
phases.objective: The live sends keep firing; the keying gets fixed so every event is deal-keyed, deduped, and renewal-safe.

rows: mid-funnel signal; skip if no deal yet
offline consult_booked keyed to deal gclid
CAPI Purchase, value = NET collected, event_id = oppId:won
offline sale_new + value, keyed to deal gclid; EC-for-Leads (hashed email/phone) fallback when no gclid
NEW ONLY - renewal Wons NEVER upload (stale click IDs train smart bidding on false signal); gated by outcome_processed_at

steps.title: Mechanism
steps.detail: Meta: relay (admin app) calls the Conversions API directly - SHA-256 hash em/ph, rebuild fbc as fb.1.{createTimestampMs}.{fbclid} from the deal's frozen fbclid, event_id as per table for dedup against the pixel. Google: the Ads API needs OAuth + developer token, which a GHL webhook action cannot carry - the relay uploads ClickConversions (gclid, conversion action, conversion_date_time, value, currency) in batches. Both send paths live in SAC (the mwcadmin server-side attribution and conversions layer), triggered by the Phase 2/3 write path - NOT by new GHL workflows.

steps.title: The one optional live-workflow edit
steps.detail: If the existing live conversion workflows (contact-field-triggered) stay on during the transition, add ONE condition to the Won-conversion workflow: new_or_renewal = the live 'new' string value - so renewals stop uploading immediately, day one, before the relay takes over. This is the only edit to any live workflow in the entire plan, and it is one if-condition. Once the relay sends are verified (Phase 6), turn the live conversion workflow's send steps off and the relay is the single sender.

steps.title: Never from SRA
steps.detail: SRA answers never upload as conversion sources and never modify click fields. Dark-channel credit (podcast, TV, billboard, referral) lives in the triangulation report where humans read it - not in smart bidding.

phases.title: Phase 6 - Verification and backfill
phases.objective: Prove the dual-write and the sends with test contacts before trusting a single report.

rows: 1 opportunity; stage won API-side; all custom fields populated; contact legacy fields identical to today's behavior; Meta Lead + Schedule + Purchase with 3 distinct event_ids; Google consult_booked + sale_new on the deal gclid; renewal_date on both objects
SECOND opportunity created (first untouched - check its PCC/amount/gclid survived); sale_type = renewal; NO Purchase upload to Meta or Google; renewal visible in internal reporting
appt_status = no-show on the deal; no outcome fields; no conversions; live no-show workflows fire exactly as today
Edit: PCC resubmits with corrected price
opportunity fields updated; outcome_processed_at unchanged; ZERO conversion re-fires; no stage re-move
one deal, one set of events - idempotency holds
contact.sra_intake_* written; no deal stamp; stamp backfilled at disposition
deal created by app path with lead_source = phone and attribution stamp from contact state
3 signature live workflows (speed-to-lead, no-show recovery, intake chase)
run a test contact through each - behavior byte-identical to before the change

steps.title: renewal_date backfill - active members only
steps.detail: Script: for every contact with an active program (legacy term/won-date fields or the disposition sheet), compute renewal_date and write contact.renewal_date (+ the open/most-recent deal if one exists). Dry-run to CSV, human-review, then commit. Without this, renewal reminders and renewal reporting start blind for the highest-LTV cohort.

steps.title: Triangulation report
steps.detail: First deliverable that pays for all of this: per deal - click lens (utm/gclid) vs intake SRA vs consult SRA. Where all three agree, attribution is settled. Where clicks say direct but both SRA lenses say podcast/TV/referral, the dark channel gets its credit. Where the lenses contradict each other, the deal flags for review instead of silently mis-crediting.

steps.title: Rollback = stop reading
steps.detail: The contact write path never changed, so every live workflow is already running the rollback state. To abandon: stop reading opportunity fields in reports and stop the relay sends. Nothing to restore, nothing to migrate back. Leave Allow Duplicate Opportunities ON (see 0.4 warning); the created deals are inert historical records.

steps.title: Keep
steps.detail: The safety snapshot from 0.2 plus per-phase commits in the repo give a paper trail for every field and every payload change.

phases.title: Phase 8 - GHL to mwcadmin (SAC) signal plane
phases.objective: SAC owns every event that leaves for Meta and Google, but it can only key events it knows about. The dual-write path covers everything the app itself writes (dispositions, creates, intake). This phase covers the events that ORIGINATE INSIDE GHL - appointments, consent changes, manual board moves - via three tiny additive signal workflows. No member messaging, no changes to existing workflows.

rows: Appointment (created / confirmed / rescheduled / cancelled / no-show status change)
Schedule conversion timing (P5), show-rate reporting, no-form chaser context, WF-free reschedule tracking
Contact DND changed / STOP reply / Customer Replied with opt-out intent
Suppression sync: SAC must never upload EC-for-Leads hashes or send CAPI events for opted-out members; also gates the triangulation report's outreach column
Opportunity status or stage changed (Modified By = User only)
Detects humans dragging deals on the board - SAC flags stage moves that bypassed the disposition form instead of silently trusting them; keeps outcome_processed_at authoritative

steps.title: Endpoint contract (mwcadmin side)
steps.detail: POST https://<mwcadmin>/api/ghl/signal. Body: {event, occurred_at (ISO), location_id, contact_id, opportunity_id?, appointment_id?, payload{...}, source: 'ghl-sig-01|02|03', sig_version: 1}. Auth: X-MWC-Signature header = HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body with a shared secret stored in GHL as a Custom Value and in mwcadmin as an env var - rotate by adding a second accepted secret, cutting workflows over, then dropping the old one. Respond 200 fast (enqueue, process async) - GHL webhook actions time out short and DO NOT RETRY.

steps.title: Idempotency and ordering (SAC side)
steps.detail: GHL appointment-triggered workflows re-enter once per appointment and can re-fire on edits, so SAC dedupes on (event, contact_id, appointment_id|opportunity_id, occurred_at bucketed to the minute) and treats signals as at-least-once. Signals are HINTS, not truth: on receipt, SAC re-reads the object via the API (PIT from P0.1) before acting - the signal says 'look here now', the API read says what is actually there. This kills every read-after-write race by construction.

steps.title: Build the three signal workflows
steps.detail: Each is one trigger + one webhook action, named per convention (workflow 'SIG-01 Appointment Signal -> mwcadmin', action 'Webhook: SIG appointment -> mwcadmin'). SIG-03 sets the trigger's Modified By filter to User so app-made and workflow-made moves do not echo back. Folder: '00 Signals'. These are ADDITIVE - they touch no existing workflow, send nothing to members, and can be turned off individually with zero blast radius.

steps.title: Reconciler stays the backstop
steps.detail: Because GHL webhook actions have no retry, the 15-minute reconciler remains the guarantee of completeness: it diffs appointments, DND state, and stage positions against SAC's last-known state and backfills anything a signal missed. Signals give SAC seconds-level latency; the reconciler gives it truth. Both, always.

steps.title: Later upgrade path (optional, not now)
steps.detail: A private marketplace OAuth app gets native webhook subscriptions (AppointmentCreate/Update, OpportunityStatusUpdate, ContactDndUpdate) with platform retries - cleaner than signal workflows, but requires app registration and OAuth plumbing. The endpoint contract in 8.1 is transport-agnostic on purpose: swapping signal workflows for app webhooks changes nothing in SAC.
